title: Position-neighborhood undercut-pressure features
summary: Official F1 strategy writeups repeatedly emphasize clean air, traffic, undercut coverage, and pit-window timing.
rationale: Official F1 strategy writeups repeatedly emphasize clean air, traffic, undercut coverage, and pit-window timing. That suggests next-lap pit probability depends on nearby rivals' state, not just the focal driver's tyre age.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x LapNumber`, build same-lap cross-sectional features: percentile ranks of tyre life/degradation/lap delta, counts of older-tyre cars ahead and fresher-tyre cars behind in `Position±2`, local median pace change, and a proxy for 'clear air if I pit now'.
expected_effect: Adds race-context signal the current single-car feature set largely misses, especially for undercut/cover calls and midfield traffic decisions.
risk: There is no explicit gap-to-car feature, so neighborhood proxies may be noisy; any same-race aggregation must stay strictly on the current lap to avoid future leakage.
